title: rubus × plicatoides
intro: Rubus × plicatoides, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a hybrid species of raspberry native to the Himalayas, resulting from the cross between Rubus armeniacus and Rubus procerus. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus × plicatoides include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Rubus × plicatoides is typically prepared for consumption as a tea, tincture, or jam, and can also be used in skincare products due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
